data.title: Walmart Sizes Up Wellness Gains With Price Cuts and GLP-1 Drug Moves
data.description:
Retail giant Walmart (WMT) is slashing prices of Wellness products and increasing access to weight-loss drugs as it looks to beef up demand from health-conscious Americans this summer.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Wellness and Weight

The company said it was launching its Walmart Wellness Day on July 25 offering customers around the country free health screenings and assessments, low-cost immunizations, pharmacist counselling and vision screenings at its stores.

This year's event, it added, will focus on overall wellness while also including weight-management resources such as GLP-1 education, nutrition guidance, medication support, information on same-day pharmacy delivery, and virtual care through its Better Care Services offering.

For those exploring GLP-1 medications or already taking them,  Walmart, from July 1, said eligible Medicare beneficiaries may be able to access certain GLP-1 weight-loss medications for $50 a month through the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge Program.

The Wellness day will also see Walmart rollback prices on fruit and organic ground beef as well as protein bars, powders, vitamins and supplements. There are also reductions on items that encourage fitness such as sneakers.

Walmart's Health Drive

Walmart has placed a large emphasis on health and wellbeing already this year.

Back in April this year Walmart said it was expanding its care services to include weight management for the millions of Americans turning to medication to control the pounds. The company said it was expanding its Better Care Services platform with new offerings to bring together virtual care, nutrition services and pharmacy access.

That means it now includes weight management support services for customers exploring GLP-1 therapies. These are either via injections or tablets, which help stimulate weight loss. Examples include Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy and Ozempic.

In May, Walmart teamed up with Virtual care provider Teladoc (TDOC) said its services, such as virtual urgent care, dermatology and nutrition services, were now available through Walmart's Better Care Services platform.

 Walmart hopes Better Care Services will help boost sales at its almost 5,000 pharmacy outlets in the U.S.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) Stocks Are Falling Today
data.description:
Shares of chipmakers Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) were falling in pre-market trading today as the selloff in technology stocks continued. Both stocks plunged more than 10% on July 1 as investors shifted money from high-growth technology stocks into more defensive sectors such as healthcare and consumer staples.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

STXX: an alternative to margin or options on STX

There was no major company-specific news that could justify the sharp price declines. Other memory and storage stocks, including Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX), also came under pressure, suggesting the selloff was sector wide.

Moreover, rising expectations of an interest rate hike this year have weighed on growth stocks. Today's June jobs data will also likely shape the Federal Reserve's next interest rate decision. Economists expect the U.S. economy to have added 115,000 jobs last month.

Outlook for Memory Chip Sector Remains Strong

Despite the recent pullback, analysts remain optimistic about the outlook for memory chip makers. Micron and SanDisk have been among the best-performing semiconductor names this year. MU stock is up nearly 262% while SNDK has surged 756%. The memory chip industry is highly cyclical, but this upcycle is being driven by tight supply and strong AI-related demand.

On Tuesday, KeyBanc analyst John Vinh said that tight memory supply and strong AI data center demand should continue supporting higher memory prices. As a result, he expects strong demand and favorable pricing for both DRAM (Dynamic Random-Access Memory) and NAND memory chips throughout 2026, helping Micron maintain solid profitability.

He added that in June, contract prices for DRAM rose about 3% from the previous month, while NAND flash memory prices increased 2.4%. The price gains reflect strong demand and tight supply across the memory market.

Meanwhile, China Renaissance analysts raised their price target on SNDK from $1,702 to $3,169, implying 55.9% upside potential from current levels. The analysts raised their earnings estimates after forecasting stronger NAND pricing, which should boost SanDisk's profitability. The firm believes stronger industry fundamentals and AI-driven storage demand should continue supporting SanDisk's earnings.

MU or SNDK: Which Is the Better Chip Stock According to Analysts?

Currently, analysts have assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating to both stocks. However, Micron offers greater upside, with an average price target implying 25.6% upside. In contrast, SanDisk's consensus target suggests about 9.3% downside after its massive rally this year.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: SpaceX Stock Price Forecast — Why Traders Are Shorting Despite a $760M Loss
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX (SPCX) is seeing a sharp rise in short seller bets following its IPO. According to Ortex data, about 196 million shares are now in short positions, representing roughly 31% of the company's free float. That is a significant jump from around 83 million shares, or 13% of the free float, just a week earlier. The stock rose at first after the IPO, but then the gains reversed. This shift shows that bearish traders are stepping in again even as early optimism fades.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

For context, short interest is the amount of a company's stock that investors have borrowed and sold short, betting the price will go down. Meanwhile, Ortex is a financial data and analytics company that tracks short interest, short selling activity, and securities lending data in real time.

Why Traders Are Shorting Despite a $760M Loss

According to Ortex estimates, short positions in SpaceX have already resulted in about $760 million in losses since the IPO. Peter Hillerberg, co-founder of Ortex, said: "The surge in short interest is extraordinary for a stock that has been listed for less than a month."

Traders are shorting mainly because many still believe the stock is overvalued. SpaceX has a very high market valuation, and some investors think the price has moved ahead of its actual near-term earnings power. Meanwhile, short sellers are also betting that the post-IPO excitement will fade. Early rallies often cool off once trading settles and buying pressure slows, which can lead to pullbacks if expectations are too high.

At the same time, SpaceX attracts strong interest from retail investors and Musk supporters. That buying demand can make shorting more risky, but it hasn't stopped traders from betting on a possible correction.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target?

SPCX stock currently carries a Moderate Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. The average price target for SpaceX is $210.86, implying a downside of 5.25% from the current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 7/2/26 – Futures Waver as Tech Rout Deepens, Jobs Data in Focus
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed ahead of Thursday's session as weakness in chip and AI-related stocks weighed on sentiment. Investors are also awaiting the June jobs report, with economists expecting the U.S. economy to have added 115,000 jobs last month.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were down 0.43% and 0.09%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.04%, at 5:31 a.m. EDT on July 2.

Oil prices remained under pressure amid easing Middle East tensions. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 0.78% at $70.35 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell 0.65% to $68.03.

During Wednesday's regular session, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.66% and the S&P 500 slipped 0.22%, weighed down by sharp declines in chip stocks. Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) plunged more than 10%, while Intel (INTC) fell over 9%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones briefly hit a new intraday record before ending little changed.

In fixed-income markets, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield stood around 4.49% as of the last check. Additionally, the spot gold price in U.S. dollars traded around $4,072.51 per ounce on Thursday.

In key economic reports, investors will monitor the June non-farm payrolls report, the unemployment rate, initial jobless claims, and May factory orders.

Elsewhere, major European indexes traded higher as gains in defensive sectors, including healthcare and consumer goods, offset weakness in AI-related technology stocks.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed as weakness in semiconductor stocks weighed on sentiment.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.76%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 2.03% while the Shenzhen Component declined 2.09%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 2.47% and the Topix closed near the flatline.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Meta's AI Compute Plan Could Spell Trouble for These 2 Stocks, Says Top Analyst
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) may reportedly start selling excess AI computing power. However, Meta has not confirmed the news. In a new report, D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria believes the move could put pressure on Nebius (NBIS) and CoreWeave (CRWV) because Meta could become both a competitor and a less reliable customer if it starts selling its own compute capacity.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

CWVX: an alternative to margin or options on CRWV

Why NBIS and CRWV Could Be at Risk

Luria, a 4.5-star analyst on TipRanks, said Meta is one of the biggest customers for both companies. According to the analyst, Meta accounts for more than half of Nebius' backlog and more than one-third of CoreWeave's backlog.

A backlog represents future work or orders that a company expects to deliver. If Meta starts using its own computing capacity instead of relying on outside providers, it may need fewer services from Nebius and CoreWeave. That could slow future growth for both companies.

Why the Move Could Benefit Meta

Meta has spent tens of billions of dollars building AI data centers and buying computing power. Luria believes selling unused computing power could help the company recover some of those costs and make better use of its existing data centers.

He also noted that Meta has previously said it could sell extra computing power if it had more than it needed. According to Luria, anything that reduces excess spending would be a positive for Meta.

The Bigger AI Picture Hasn't Changed

Despite the potential impact on some cloud providers, Luria remains bullish on AI infrastructure demand. He believes demand for AI computing still exceeds supply, with companies such as Anthropic continuing to need additional computing power.

Luria also said the report should not be viewed as a negative signal for the broader AI chip market. While companies like Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Broadcom (AVGO) could see some impact if AI infrastructure spending slows, he believes Nvidia (NVDA) remains in a strong position because demand for its chips continues to outpace supply.

Which Stock Offers the Highest Upside?

According to TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, IREN offers the highest upside among the six stocks, with an average analyst price target implying about 73% upside. Nvidia ranks second with roughly 57% upside, followed by CoreWeave at about 54%.

Meanwhile, analysts see Broadcom and Meta gaining about 39% and 33%, respectively. AMD is the only stock in the group with a consensus price target below its current share price, implying about 9% downside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Strategy Stock Soars as BTIG Analysts Say  'MSTR's Capital Structure Will Enable It to Outperform BTC'
data.description:
Financial firm BTIG just released a new update on Strategy stock (MSTR), giving crypto investors a major reason to look forward to the future. Even though the digital asset market has faced a tough drop recently, BTIG analysts Andrew Harte and Brendan Greaney are urging people to focus on the long-term potential of the company. In their research note from July 1, 2026, the team stated, "we are positive on BTC long-term and believe MSTR's capital structure will enable it to outperform BTC as prices recover".

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

MSTR stock closed up 7.43% yesterday and jumped another 6.37% in early pre-market trading today as investors enthusiastically reacted to Strategy's new capital management plan.

New Framework Protects Strategy Preferred Shares

In their report, the analysts highlighted that Strategy's preferred shares, especially STRC, surged about 12% following the announcement of its new financial framework. The team pointed out that this new plan focuses on two main improvements: keeping enough cash on hand to cover at least a year of interest and dividend costs, and setting up share buybacks to help the company trade above its actual net asset value.

Harte and Greaney explained that while Strategy holds an enormous amount of crypto, enough to cover its preferred payouts for the next 28 years, the market cares much more about actual cash safety right now. To fix this concern, the business raised over $1 billion by selling common stock last week, building up a U.S. dollar reserve that safely covers 17 months of dividends.

BTIG Updates Its MSTR Stock Price Target

Even though the team remains highly optimistic about the future, they had to lower their price target from $350 down to $250. This change simply accounts for the 28% drop in Bitcoin's price (BTC-USD) since the first quarter, alongside the company's upcoming dividend obligations.

Despite the lower price target, the firm kept its strong Buy rating. Harte and Greaney concluded that they view Strategy's shift from a simple "one-way capital issuance story" to an active manager of its own funds as a highly positive near-term spark for the stock.

Is Strategy Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

According to TipRanks, Strategy (formerly known as MicroStrategy) stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 12 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 10 Buy and two Hold ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month MSTR price target of $291.92 implies 212.6% upside from current levels. (See MSTR stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: SpaceX Stock (SPCX) Craters 7% as Musk Calls WSJ Al Device Report "Utterly False"
data.description:
Shares of aerospace giant SpaceX (SPCX) fell sharply after the Wall Street Journal reported a false story about the firm. The news media said SpaceX gave investors a prototype of an AI-powered handheld device before its public debut. 

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

High conviction on SPCX? Try Tradr's SPCM or SPCG

Founder and CEO Elon Musk quickly debunked the report, calling it "utterly false" in a post on X. Despite the denial, investors continued to sell the stock. Following this, SpaceX shares dropped over 7.8% at the close on Wednesday.

Musk Rejects WSJ Report of AI Device Prototype

On July 1, the WSJ reported that SpaceX had shown an early prototype of an AI-powered handheld device to some investors and partners before its initial public offering (IPO). The report said the device would use SpaceX's own operating system and AI tech from xAI, Musk's artificial intelligence firm, and chips from Qualcomm (QCOM), a U.S.-based semiconductor maker.

The report also stated that the project was still in its early stages, with the design still under development. It further noted that there is no assurance that the device would ever reach the market.

A few hours after the publication, Musk responded on X, calling the WSJ report "utterly false." He left it at that and did not provide further details on the matter. Also, neither SpaceX nor Qualcomm commented further on the report.

Despite Musk's quick denial, SpaceX stock crashed 7.8% on Wednesday, extending its decline over the past few weeks. TipRanks data also show that the stock is down more than 0.18% in premarket trading on Thursday, with the price hovering around $157.54 as investors remain cautious about the rumors.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Michael Saylor Just Blew Up His Own 'Never Sell' Philosophy to Save Strategy Stock. Here's Why.
data.description:
For years, Strategy (MSTR) founder Michael Saylor was the ultimate champion of a crypto rule: buy Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and never sell it. His company hoarded nearly 850,000 Bitcoin, turning the firm into the largest corporate holder of the digital asset on Earth. Saylor famously preached that Bitcoin was a permanent store of value that should be locked away forever. But a harsh crypto winter has forced a Michael Saylor to blow up his own "never sell" crypto philosophy.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

With Bitcoin sitting more than 50% below its record high, Strategy has officially abandoned its strict buy-only rule. The company's board just approved a new financial system called the Digital Credit Capital Framework. For the very first time, this plan gives management permission to sell up to $1.25 billion worth of its Bitcoin stockpile. The company plans to use the cash from these sales to build a U.S. dollar reserve, pay out dividends to investors, and handle its corporate debts.

There Are Limits to Holding Forever

This policy shift reveals a major flaw in Saylor's original vision. While treating a volatile cryptocurrency like a permanent piggy bank sounds great during a market boom, real-world businesses have fixed bills that cannot be paid in crypto. Strategy faced growing pressure as its stock price tumbled near a 52-week low. Investors have been pulling away from crypto to chase the booming artificial intelligence market.

To make matters worse, a crash in Strategy's preferred stock created a funding crisis. The company owes massive annual dividend and interest payments of about $1.76 billion. Because it ran low on cash, the firm actually quietly sold 32 Bitcoin in May, just to pay its bills. That small sale was a clear warning sign. Now, by opening the door to billion-dollar liquidations, the business is admitting that a pure "never sell" strategy is impossible to maintain when a market downturn hits hard.

Strategy Aims to Protect the Corporate Structure

To stop its stock from sliding further, Strategy is launching a $2 billion buyback program. The firm will split that money evenly, spending $1 billion to buy back its common stock and another $1 billion to rescue its struggling preferred shares. Management explains that this move turns the business into an active capital manager rather than a passive crypto vault.

Wall Street actually cheered the announcement, sending the stock up over 11% as investors felt relieved that the company is taking steps to stay stable. Still, the irony is hard to ignore. Michael Saylor spent years telling the world that cash was trash and Bitcoin was the only asset worth holding. Now, his own company is selling off its precious Bitcoin just to stack up billions of plain old U.S. dollars.

Is Strategy Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

According to TipRanks, Strategy (formerly known as MicroStrategy) stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 12 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 10 Buy and two Hold ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month MSTR price target of $291.92 implies 212.6% upside from current levels. (See MSTR stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Bull vs. Bear: Is CoreWeave Stock at Risk From Meta's Cloud Biz? Analysts Offer Contrasting Takes
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) stock took a heavy beating on Wednesday after reports emerged that Meta is planning to launch a new cloud business.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

Explore CWVX for 2X leverage on CRWV

The initiative would see the company monetize surplus computing capacity by selling it to external customers, helping to offset the multibillion-dollar investments it has made in AI infrastructure. The move would place Meta in the highly competitive cloud segment, putting it in direct competition with established hyperscalers such as Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, as well as neoclouds like CoreWeave.

Investors obviously thought this was bad news for CoreWeave, and so does Bernstein analyst Madison Rezaei, even though Meta has yet to confirm the news.

While there is certainly room for debate around the "will-they-won't-they" question, what is clear, says Rezaei, is that Meta already operates one of the largest data center footprints globally. Rezaei estimates it has roughly 20 GW worldwide today, with an additional approximately 14 GW expected to come online over the next few years across both owned and leased assets. At this scale, its infrastructure is easily comparable to that of major cloud providers. "If Meta is developing plans for an externally-facing cloud infrastructure business that could compete head-on with AWS, Azure, GCP… it's problematic for CoreWeave," the analyst opined.

Rezaei has regularly argued that it is only a matter of time before hyperscalers begin competing directly with CoreWeave. Meta currently holds approximately $35.2 billion of CoreWeave contracts, accounting for just over one-third of CoreWeave's backlog. When combined with roughly $14 billion from Microsoft, close to half of its backlog is tied to customers that will effectively be direct competitors at the point of renewal.

It's true that existing contracts are "tight and difficult to churn out of," so Rezaei does not see an immediate threat to CoreWeave's financial position or its debt profile. However, she views this as "extremely consistent" with her core thesis: larger, better-capitalized competitors are likely to enter the GPUaaS market, and over time CoreWeave could both lose existing customers at renewal and face increasing difficulty winning new business.

"To us," Rezaei summed up, "this adds to what we've already seen from Google-Blackstone, Colossus, and other fringe players – we anticipate competition will only heat up with time and the CRWV business model will be unsustainable."

Accordingly, Rezaei reiterated an Underperform rating on CRWV shares, while her $67 price target points toward further losses of 22% in the months ahead. (To watch Rezaei's track record, click here)

So, that's a bear's view, and one Rosenblatt analyst John McPeake disagrees with. The analyst's checks indicate no change in hyperscaler behavior regarding GPU compute procurement, with GPU shortages remaining the norm at the moment across the industry. McPeake also does not believe Meta has the contractual right to resell any of the capacity it has leased from CoreWeave to third parties through 2032.

In fact, McPeake thinks investors should make the most of the share price drop, believing the weakness in CRWV stock "provides a buying opportunity."

To this end, McPeake maintained a Buy rating on the shares, backed by a Street-high $250 price target, implying the stock will gain 192% over the next year. (To watch McPeake's track record, click here)

So, those are entirely different takes, but where does the rest of the Street stand? Based on an additional 13 Buys and 9 Holds, the stock claims a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The forecast calls for 12-month returns of 54.5%, considering the average target clocks in at $132.35. (See CRWV stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
